--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,59 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: a380
+project: file-edit-tools
+title: write files should clobber existing files — drop (or escape-hatch) the read-before-write freshness guard
+---
+## Problem
+
+`write files` (the `write` op on the `files` MCP tool) refuses to overwrite an existing file unless the caller passes a matching `expected_hash` freshness token. When the token is **absent or stale**, the tool does NOT write — instead it returns the file's *current* content (hashline-tagged, led by a `#hash:` line) as a **SUCCESS** (`is_error: false`, no `bytes_written`).
+
+To a calling agent this is indistinguishable from a successful write at a glance and reads like the tool is broken. A real agent report:
+
+> "The write file op is returning the file unchanged (read-style output, no mutation/bytes_written) — it looks like it's refusing to clobber the existing file. Let me try forcing overwrite."
+
+The agent had no `force` option and was left confused. Agents that call `write files` on an existing path overwhelmingly *intend* to clobber (regenerating a whole file). We have source control as the safety net for lost work, so the lost-update protection the guard buys is not worth the friction + confusion it creates.
+
+## Current design (for context — this is deliberate, documented behavior)
+
+- Impl: `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/files/write/mod.rs`
+  - `execute_write` calls `freshness_rebase(&validated_path, expected_hash)` when `validated_path.exists()`. On divergence it returns `BaseToolImpl::create_success_response(rebase)` — the silent no-op-as-success path.
+  - New / nonexistent files are unguarded and write freely.
+- Param: `expected_hash` in `WRITE_FILE_PARAMS`.
+- Docs: `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/files/write/description.md` ("Read-before-write freshness guard").
+- Design origin: `ideas/file-edit-tools.md` ("**`write files` — read-before-write guard.**") — explicitly meant to mirror the hashline read-before-edit mandate and compose with the closed-write-surface goal.
+
+Note: the guard is **purely lost-update protection**. It is NOT required for the closed-write-surface / inline-diagnostics goal (`doc/src/concepts/closed-write-surface.md`) — those flow from the write going through the instrumented tool at all, regardless of the hash check. So dropping the guard does not weaken diagnostics.
+
+## Decision
+
+Make `write files` **clobber an existing file by default**. The whole point of `write` is whole-file replacement; source control is the recovery path. Remove the silent divergence-as-success behavior — that's the worst part: a write the agent believes happened silently didn't.
+
+Concretely (proposed):
+1. Drop the freshness guard from the `write` op so an existing file is overwritten like a new one. Keep atomic temp-and-rename.
+2. Remove the `expected_hash` param + `freshness_rebase` + `HASH_LINE_PREFIX` (or keep `expected_hash` accepted-and-ignored for one release if any caller passes it — verify none do).
+3. A successful overwrite carries the normal mutation envelope (`tagged_content` + `mutated_paths` + `bytes_written`), same as a new-file write.
+4. Update `description.md` and the `ideas/file-edit-tools.md` write-op note to match.
+
+## Alternatives considered (rejected unless the implementer finds a blocker)
+
+- **Keep guard, make divergence a hard ERROR** (`is_error: true` with a clear "file exists & changed; re-read or pass expected_hash" message). Fixes the *confusion* but keeps the friction; agents still can't just overwrite. Weaker than the decision given the user's stance.
+- **Add an explicit `overwrite: true` / `force` escape hatch, guard on by default.** Honors both protection and intent, but adds a flag the model must learn and the default still bites first-call. The user's directive ("they do want to clobber, and we have source control") favors clobber-by-default, not opt-in.
+
+If the implementer believes lost-update protection must be retained, the fallback is the `overwrite`-flag option — but default behavior MUST be a real clobber, never a silent no-op-as-success.
+
+## Tests to update / add (`write/mod.rs` test module)
+
+- `test_write_overwrite_existing_file` — must overwrite WITHOUT any `expected_hash`.
+- DELETE / invert the guard tests: `test_write_existing_file_without_hash_does_not_clobber`, `test_write_existing_file_with_stale_hash_does_not_clobber`, `guard_divergence_write_has_no_mutation_envelope`, `test_write_existing_file_with_matching_hash_succeeds` (token no longer required).
+- Add: overwriting an existing file (no token) returns the mutation envelope and the new bytes are on disk.
+- Keep: new-file write, parent-dir creation, size limit, readonly-fails, atomic-cleanup, unicode.
+
+## Acceptance criteria
+
+- `write files` on an existing path with no `expected_hash` overwrites the file and returns `is_error: false` with a mutation envelope (`bytes_written` > 0, `mutated_paths` set).
+- No code path returns the file's current content as a "success" without having written.
+- `description.md` + `ideas/file-edit-tools.md` updated; no dangling references to `expected_hash` / freshness guard for write.
+- `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-tools` green.
\ No newline at end of file
